text.title: Scrum Ceremonies Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the scrum-ceremonies methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; the final rule defines when to skip this methodology entirely.

rule.statement: Sprint goal MUST be one sentence falsifiable at review (binary success/fail). Vague goals like 'make progress on X' MUST be rejected at planning.
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.

rule.statement: Every retro action item MUST have a named owner + linked issue + due date — or it is excluded from the actions log.
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.

rule.statement: Sprint Planning MUST NOT start without a refined-Ready backlog of ≥1.5× sprint capacity; force a refinement session first when below threshold.
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.

rule.statement: Standup output MUST be delivered to the team channel — not to a management dashboard — to preserve self-organisation.

rationale.source: Psychological safety research; internal trust surveys

rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.

rule.statement: Async standup blockers older than 1 working day MUST escalate to sync or named owner — never rot in a thread.
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.

rule.statement: Skip when team size <3 or no incremental delivery cadence — fall back to Kanban or Lean.
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.
